skill.name: firebase-basics
skill.description: Guides agents through Firebase environment setup, CLI authentication, project selection, service initialization, SDK setup, and agent-specific Firebase MCP configuration.
skill.summary: Set up and operate Firebase projects with CLI, SDK, and MCP guidance.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static findings are mostly true positives for documented command execution, network access, filesystem configuration, and Firebase API key placeholders, but most are legitimate for a Firebase setup skill. Human review is still required because the skill contains prompt-control language and instructs agents to install remote packages and modify local AI agent configuration files.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Style Agent Control
high_findings.description: The skill uses broad agent-control language that tells agents to always use the skill for Firebase and not proceed with Firebase tasks until setup is complete. This does not show confirmed malicious intent, but it can override normal task prioritization and requires marketplace review.
high_findings.recommendation: Rewrite these instructions as scoped guidance for Firebase setup, and remove language that claims mandatory agent authority.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain explicit always-use and do-not-proceed agent directives. They do not target the evaluator or security review, so confidence is high but not critical.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Execution Through CLI Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs agents to run npx commands that fetch the latest Firebase tools or skills packages. This is expected for Firebase setup, but it creates supply-chain and unattended execution risk.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require user confirmation before running package manager commands and prefer pinned versions where practical.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and semantically intended to execute remote package tooling. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it remains a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Agent Configuration File Modification
medium_findings.description: The setup guides instruct agents to locate, create, and merge MCP server configuration into local or home-directory agent settings. This is legitimate setup behavior, but incorrect edits could alter trusted tool configuration.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require an explicit user approval step before editing agent configuration, and preserve existing MCP server entries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly direct agents to modify MCP configuration files, including hidden home-directory paths. The instructions also say to preserve existing servers, reducing but not eliminating risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are links to official or relevant setup documentation and repository locations. They do not show hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep external links visible and user-facing, and avoid adding hidden network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly shown in Markdown setup instructions. No evidence found that these links transmit local data or credentials.

low_findings.title: Firebase Web API Key Placeholder
low_findings.description: The env_access finding points to an example Firebase web configuration placeholder. Firebase web API keys identify a project but are not service account secrets by themselves.
low_findings.recommendation: Clarify that users should not place service account keys or private credentials in client-side Firebase configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value is a placeholder inside a standard Firebase client initialization example. No evidence found of real credentials or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to be false positives triggered by ordinary words and command text in setup documentation. No evidence found of implemented cryptographic code.
low_findings.recommendation: No cryptography remediation is needed unless future versions add actual hashing or encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown setup guidance, not executable crypto implementation. The static pattern likely matched substrings such as skill names or command arguments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Latest-Version Package Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill normalizes commands that execute the latest Firebase CLI through npx. This can run newly published package code without version pinning.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Prefer pinned versions for repeatable automation, or ask the user before using latest packages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package execution pattern is directly documented and repeated. The risk is supply-chain exposure, not proven malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Firebase setup often fails because tools, login state, and project context are not ready. This skill guides agents through CLI setup, authentication, project selection, SDK initialization, and MCP configuration.
actual_capabilities: Checks Node.js and Firebase CLI readiness before Firebase work begins.
Guides Firebase login and active project selection through CLI commands.
Explains project creation and Firebase service initialization workflows.
Provides Firebase web app SDK setup guidance.
Documents MCP setup for Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Antigravity, and other agents.
Provides refresh steps for keeping Firebase agent skills updated.
limitations: Requires Firebase CLI, Node.js, and user authentication to perform most tasks.
Includes commands that may install or update packages from remote registries.
Does not replace Firebase product-specific security rules or architecture review.
May require manual confirmation when browser login or agent restart is needed.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Firebase Development Environment
use_cases.description: Verify Node.js, Firebase CLI, authentication, and active project context before starting implementation.

use_cases.title: Configure Firebase MCP Access
use_cases.description: Add Firebase MCP server settings for an AI coding agent while preserving existing configuration.

use_cases.title: Initialize Firebase Services
use_cases.description: Create a project, register a web app, install the SDK, and initialize Firebase services.

prompt_templates.title: Check Firebase Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this workspace is ready for Firebase development. Verify Node.js, Firebase CLI, login state, and active project context.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Firebase Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a new Firebase project from the CLI and setting it as the active project for this workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Add Firebase to a Web App
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me register a Firebase web app, install the Firebase SDK, and create the initialization file for this project.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Agent MCP Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current AI agent environment and guide me through adding the Firebase MCP server without overwriting existing servers.

output_examples.input: Check if I can use Firebase in this repo.
output_examples.output: A readiness checklist covering Node.js version, Firebase CLI availability, login status, active project, and any setup steps still required.

output_examples.input: Set up Firebase for my web app.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan to create or select a project, register a web app, install the SDK, fetch config, and initialize Firebase services.

output_examples.input: Add Firebase MCP to my coding agent.
output_examples.output: Agent-specific guidance that identifies the right configuration file, checks for an existing Firebase server, and explains the merge needed.

best_practices: Ask before running package installation, login, or configuration-changing commands.
Preserve existing MCP server entries when updating agent configuration files.
Use official Firebase CLI and MCP tooling before relying on general web search.
anti_patterns: Running latest remote packages without user awareness or approval.
Overwriting an existing MCP configuration file instead of merging changes.
Treating Firebase client config placeholders as private service account secrets.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy Firebase apps?
faq.answer: It provides setup and CLI guidance. Deployment still depends on the project, selected Firebase services, and user approval.

faq.question: Does it require Firebase authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. Most project operations require a Firebase login with permissions for the target project.

faq.question: Can it configure Firebase MCP for different agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes setup guidance for Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Antigravity, and other agents.

faq.question: Are Firebase web API keys treated as secrets?
faq.answer: No. Firebase web API keys identify a project, but service account keys and private credentials must stay secret.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked high risk?
faq.answer: It includes broad agent-control language and commands that install remote packages or edit local agent configuration.

faq.question: Is the skill blocked as malicious?
faq.answer: No. The audit found no confirmed malicious intent, but publication should wait for human review.
